blocks.content: Wednesday after school. We walked the long way without deciding to — it just happened. The air was cold. She had her bag on one shoulder and was telling me about something her English teacher said and I wasn't tracking all of it but I was tracking her.
At some point my hand found hers. I didn't plan it. She didn't flinch or make it a moment. She just kept talking and shifted her fingers so they fit right.
That was it. The whole thing. Just two people walking.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I'm present. I'm just — present differently.
She laughed — the real one, quick and not dressed up. And then she squeezed my hand once and kept walking.

I didn't see Jonathan until we turned the corner. He was coming from the other direction with Marcus, and he clocked it immediately. Not the hand-holding — he clocked my face. Then the hands. Then back to my face.
He didn't say anything. He gave me a look that communicated approximately seven thousand things and then just kept walking like he hadn't seen anything at all.
We separated at the end of the block. Crystal went her way, I went mine.

blocks.prompt: Jonathan saw. What does that mean?

options.label: Good. He's been waiting for this longer than Larry has.
That was supposed to be private. Not secret — just private.
